jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 7+ years of front-end technologist, engineer, or UX prototyper experience
- Have an available online portfolio
- Have coding samples in front end programming languages
- Experience developing visually polished, engaging, and highly fluid UX prototypes
- Experience collaborating with UX, Product, and technical partners
jobs.description:
Are you interested in shaping the future of entertainment? Do you want to define the next generation of how and what Amazon customers are watching? Prime Video is changing the way people watch movies and TV. From one service, we’re delivering versatile ways to watch hundreds of thousands of movies, TV series, and live events on the web, mobile, and TV. Our service is available in more than 200 territories globally, and we’re just getting started.

Working closely with business and technology partners, we use research, design, and prototyping to guide early product development, bring clarity to engineering goals, and develop a customer-centric point of view. This is where you come in.

As a Senior Design Technologist, you'll be a key part of our Experience Design organization. Your role will be all about proving, advancing, and evolving forward-looking UX concepts through prototyping and hands-on product work. You'll work alongside other design technologists to bring design ideas (interactions, visuals, motion) to life.


Key job responsibilities
- Build high-quality, functional prototypes that convey and advance the vision of future products
- Collaborate with design leadership and key stakeholders throughout the product lifecycle
- Work alongside designers, researchers, PMs, and engineers to drive concepts from idea to implementation
- Advance design thinking by leveraging your platform expertise (e.g. web, mobile, TV)
- Thrive in a fast-paced, iterative environment with ambiguous challenges and brand new customer experiences

A day in the life
As a Senior Design Technologist at Prime Video, you’ll have the opportunity to wear many hats. We build prototypes across the Prime Video customer journey from helping customers discover their favorite content to designing new playback experiences including X-Ray, live sports, and advertising. Our team builds high-fidelity prototypes for TV, mobile and web clients using frontend technologies such as HTML/CSS/JS, React.js, Redux and Motion.js. Our team develops prototypes using AWS Bedrock to envision brand new Gen AI powered experiences for our customers.

About the team
We’re scrappy yet detailed-oriented, knowing when to build quickly versus diving into the details to produce higher fidelity prototypes. We bring our own design vision and support our design and engineering partners to help them ship faster. We also partner closely with UX researchers to create new experiences that our customers get to test and provide feedback on. Our prototypes are demoed to senior leadership to showcase what the future of Prime Video will look and feel like for our customers.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in management, business administration, economics, engineering, marketing
- 3+ years of program or project management experience
- 3+ years of advanced Microsoft Word and Excel experience
- Experience in managing multiple, competing priorities simultaneously
- Proficiency with project management and collaboration tools (e.g., Sharepoint, Airtable, Asana)
- Experience implementing AI-powered tools or automation solutions to streamline research processes and reduce manual tasks
- Experience with survey tool platforms (e.g., Qualtrics, Alida) with ability to navigate these tools to access data for analysis
- Experience with syndicated research tools (Nielsen, Kantar, IRI) for accessing and analyzing insights
jobs.description:
Are you passionate about understanding customers and want to help deliver high-quality research? Join Amazon's Worldwide Grocery Stores (WWGS) team and play a critical role in making research accessible and actionable! As a Research Program Manager supporting both Consumer Insights and User Experience Research teams within WWGS, you'll ensure our research is well-organized, easily discoverable, and effectively communicated across the organization. You'll gain exposure to research methodologies across two complementary disciplines while building the infrastructure that enables great research programs.

Key job responsibilities
- Own the strategic development and optimization of the centralized research knowledge repository and Airtable project management system, implementing scalable processes and governance frameworks that enable efficient insight discovery and project tracking across the team
- Serve as primary point of contact for performance tracker, maintaining consolidated CSAT dashboards, triaging requests, and routing complex projects to senior researchers
- Partner with senior researchers to conduct knowledge reviews for stakeholders by synthesizing insights from the research repository and syndicated studies, delivering comprehensive summaries that answer business questions and identify relevant past research to reduce redundant research requests
- Design and maintain standardized research templates, best practice guides, and process documentation that ensure consistency, quality, and efficiency across team deliverables
- Lead outbound tracker recruitment campaigns, monitoring campaign performance and troubleshooting issues with partner teams to ensure consistent customer feedback collection
- Partner with third-party research vendors to manage project timelines, ensure deliverable quality, and coordinate learning events
- Collaborate with senior researchers to optimize AI-powered tools and integrations, identifying opportunities to improve document retrieval and implementing enhancements that accelerate insight generation


A day in the life
Your day includes optimizing the research repository architecture to improve discoverability, maintaining the Airtable project management tool, and building executive-ready dashboards that visualize CSAT and Brand Tracker data. You'll collaborate with senior researchers to enhance AI-powered tools—identifying opportunities to improve how systems retrieve documents and implementing enhancements that accelerate insight generation. You'll coordinate logistics for upcoming studies—from vendor timelines to recruitment campaigns—while navigating survey platforms to access data for analysis and accessing data from syndicated research tools to help create more holistic insights for knowledge reviews. Throughout the day, you're maintaining wiki documentation on research processes while building the operational infrastructure that makes research accessible and actionable across WWGS.
jobs.description_short: Are you passionate about understanding customers and want to help deliver high-quality research? Join Amazon's Worldwide Grocery Stores (WWGS) team and play a critical role in making research accessible

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of customer-facing work, engaging with customer executives, technologists or partners to solve business problems with advanced technologies experience
- Experience implementing cloud services including migrations and modernization projects or similar
- Experience in written and verbal communication with the ability to present complex technical information in a clear and concise manner to executives and non-technical leaders
- Travel up to 25% of the time
- 3+ years of hands-on experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure, or similar), including infrastructure, compute, storage, networking, and managed services
- Working knowledge of at least one competing cloud platform (GCP, Azure) with the ability to map services and identify migration complexity across platforms
- Experience managing a portfolio of customer engagements with defined revenue, adoption, or delivery targets — with a track record of using data to prioritize and drive outcomes
- Ability to manage multiple customer engagements simultaneously in a fast-paced, high-autonomy environment
jobs.description:
Startups are future large enterprises, built by people who want to create something that matters. They're usually short on capital, engineers, or experience — sometimes all three.

When they commit to migrating to AWS, often off another cloud, the pressure is real. They need someone who can help them move fast, get value out of the migration quickly, and turn that investment into numbers they can point to.

That's this role. As a Sr. Customer Solutions Manager on the AWS Startups team, you own migration revenue realization for a portfolio of startup customers. You make sure migrations launch on time, workloads cut over without stalling, and the revenue they generate tracks against forecast. You work directly with founders, CTOs, and engineering leads, and you coordinate across AWS internal teams, partners, and migration programs to make sure every deal in your portfolio delivers.

This role is metric-driven and operationally rigorous. You need technical depth, program management discipline, and an intuition for how startups operate. You'll use revenue trend data, dynamic forecasting, and portfolio-level inspection to decide where to focus — unsticking stalled migrations, reversing revenue declines, and pushing growth where there's momentum.

Key job responsibilities
Migration Revenue Realization & Portfolio Management

Own migration revenue realization outcomes for a portfolio of startup migration deals, ensuring attainment against forecasted revenue targets across eligible migration programs.

Inspect portfolio health using dynamic revenue forecasting and trend categorization — prioritizing intervention on migrations that have not launched, have stopped spending, or are experiencing revenue decline, before focusing on accelerating growth

Monitor and optimize revenue telemetry for each migration type in your portfolio, understanding the specific tracking mechanism and ensuring compliance with requirements and guidelines

Triage and resolve migration blockers using proven accelerators: driving Experienced Based Accelerators for quick starts, implementing accelerated onboarding and initiation processes, optimizing deal scope prior to winning, and ensuring strategic partner engagement

Coordinate with Partner Delivery teams on partner-funded migrations within your portfolio, ensuring alignment on migration success activities and revenue realization outcomes

Customer Engagement & Migration Execution

Engage directly with startup leadership to understand their technical architecture, business priorities, growth trajectory, and migration drivers — translating these into actionable migration strategies that align with revenue realization timelines

Conduct architectural assessments and service mapping, identifying source-platform service equivalents on AWS and designing migration paths that align with the customer's engineering culture and release cadence

Accelerate migration velocity by connecting customers with the right AWS resources, tools, and programs at the right time: removing friction and keeping momentum high

Drive cost optimization from day one, helping customers leverage AWS pricing models, Savings Plans, Reserved Instances, Spot, and Graviton to maximize value — recognizing that cost efficiency directly impacts both customer satisfaction and sustained post-migration spend

Credibly articulate AWS differentiation in the context of the customer's current platform, addressing technical concerns, performance comparisons, and ecosystem advantages with depth and honesty

Strategic & Cross-Functional

Coordinate across AWS internal teams (account teams, solutions architects, migration program teams, service teams, support) to deliver a seamless customer experience — absorbing internal process complexity and organizational boundaries so they are not exposed to the customer

Serve as the voice of startup migration customers back into AWS, advocating with product, program, and service teams to improve the migration experience and close gaps that matter to startups switching platforms

Create and share migration-focused content — playbooks, case studies, best practices — that helps the broader startup community navigate their journey to AWS

Identify post-migration adoption opportunities, helping customers deepen their use of AWS services beyond the initial migration scope — driving sustained and growing revenue post-migration

About the team
The AWS Startups team works exclusively with startups. We get how they operate: speed matters, every dollar counts, engineering teams are small, and the tech decisions made today shape where the company ends up in three years. The migration CSM function exists to make sure that when a startup commits to AWS, they get the full value of that commitment fast — not six months from now.
jobs.description_short: Startups are future large enterprises, built by people who want to create something that matters. They're usually short on capital, engineers, or experience — sometimes all three. When they commit to migrating

jobs.description:
Amazon Advertising is growing its online advertising program and is seeking an Ad Ops Services Associate, to support our Global business. The Ad Ops Services Associate will provide world class customer service and support for the Amazon global advertising business. Responsibilities include the daily operations of advertising campaigns across Amazon and its subsidiaries owned and operated sites. This role will be responsible for the success of their own campaigns as well as the success of associates who work within their queue. This includes accurate building, testing and assignment of advertisements using various ad serving technologies.

NOTE: This is a seasonal temporary role.

This person will also assist in providing support and coverage to global advertising teams as well as ensuring accuracy and security of all ad units.


Responsibilities include:

- Maintain the highest standards around campaign fulfillment
- Triage campaigns daily across multiple team members, ensuring proper bandwidth allocation
- Manage platform queue success ensuring campaigns are within SLA and accurate
- Execute basic campaigns by trafficking ads to Amazon's stable of properties: Amazon, IMDb, DPreview.com, etc.
- Build creative in an ad server using third party tags
- Activate ads in the ad server after assigning the appropriate creative
- Communicating status of campaign creation to key global partners
- Provide support and coverage to other advertising teams
- Provide account team with screenshots confirming creative went live
- Complete ad server and campaign audits using checklists to ensure accuracy and completeness of campaign, including go live workflows
- Ensure functionality and accuracy of creative by assigning to test pages
- Assign creative to orders within out ad serving systems
- Security scanning of orders to ensure compliance with information security initiatives
- Provide, verify 3rd party reporting
- Uphold user experience by confirming ad units when they go live

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in engineering or equivalent, or experience in software development
- 10+ years of technical program management experience in engineering-intensive roles, leading complex, cross-functional programs in technology, aerospace, satellite systems, or related technical domains; with a strong personal engineering background enabling hands-on technical contributions and architecture guidance
- Demonstrated experience owning system architecture and managing programs spanning hardware, software, and algorithm development with multiple interdependent teams and technical disciplines; ability to review and contribute meaningfully to engineering design documents, architecture decision records, and technical specifications
- Proven track record of defining and delivering technically complex strategic programs with broad cross-organizational or cross-regional business impact that solve significantly complex or endemic engineering problems
- Experience operating with complete independence in highly ambiguous environments where technical direction, system boundaries, and business and program strategy are not yet defined
- Deep technical expertise in system architectures, distributed satellite systems, navigation algorithms, clock synchronization, optical communications, ground infrastructure, and satellite constellation operations — at a level sufficient to personally drive technical decisions, author architecture documents, and evaluate engineering trade-offs
- Exceptional written and verbal communication skills with demonstrated ability to present system architecture diagrams, engineering trade-off analyses, and complex technical information, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to VP and SVP-level audiences
- Experience influencing technical roadmaps, organizational priorities, securing resources, and negotiating with senior leadership without direct authority
jobs.description:
Amazon Leo is building a constellation of Low Earth Orbit satellites to provide low-latency, high-speed broadband connectivity to unserved and underserved communities around the world. We are seeking an exceptional Principal Technical Program Manager to serve as the single-threaded technical owner for the delivery of a resilient Position, Navigation, and Timing (PNT).

This is a high-visibility, high-impact role requiring deep technical leadership and strategic leadership across multiple analysis, software, and hardware teams within Amazon Leo. You will own the technical architecture and manage a program with contributions from all Amazon Leo elements including customer terminals, ground gateways, satellites, optical inter-satellite links and Amazon Leo Government.

The ideal candidate thrives in ambiguity, operates with complete independence, and has a proven track record of delivering complex, cross-organizational technical projects that solve critical technical and business challenges. You will work directly with engineering leaders, product managers, regulatory teams, and senior leadership to drive technical clarity, architect solutions, make strategic trade-offs, and ensure successful delivery of this transformative capability for Amazon Leo.

Export Control Requirement:
Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

Key job responsibilities
● Own end-to-end resilient PNT system design, architecture, program plan, roadmap, and execution; make authoritative technical decisions and drive architecture trade-offs across hardware, software, and algorithm domains
● Lead technical design reviews, define system interfaces, and establish engineering standards; own delivery of software and hardware products, and overall capability for resilient PNT
● Manage program lifecycle from concept through systems engineering, design, build, integration & test, and continuous operations; apply hands-on technical judgment at every phase to resolve integration challenges and technical blockers
● Model excellence in technical program management practices including communications, planning, scheduling, critical path identification, risk management, and escalation; translate complex engineering trade-spaces into clear program decisions
● Manage cross-functional technical communications across engineering, product, business development, regulatory, and operations teams to ensure stakeholder alignment and technically informed decision-making
● Identify and tackle intrinsically hard engineering and systems problems with significant business impact, operating with complete autonomy and demonstrating high judgment in decisions with architectural and business implications
● Build consensus and align engineering teams across multiple VP organizations toward coherent technical strategies and simple, effective approaches; influence organizational priorities and system technology direction
● Create and optimize critical mechanisms to ensure long-term program success; establish clear, measurable technical and success criteria and drive data-driven decision-making grounded in engineering metrics and system performance indicators
● Proactively identify technical risks and opportunities in strategies and organizational structures; develop mitigation plans before risks become roadblocks; manage executive-level escalations with excellent technical and judgment
● Drive productive discussions with senior leadership to secure resources, negotiate priorities, and align on strategic direction; present program status and system architecture analyses, technical trade-offs, and recommendations to executive audiences

jobs.description:
Amazon is seeking a Senior Tax Analyst to join its direct tax reporting team in Hyderabad, India. This position will be responsible for tax reporting and tax compliance of Amazon’s legal entities located in the EMEA region.



Key job responsibilities
Working within the EMEA Foreign Reporting & Compliance team, the Senior Tax Analyst will be required to:
• Prepare / Review foreign income tax returns and other foreign tax filings and supporting work papers
• Prepare / Review foreign income tax provisions under IFRS and/or Local GAAP
• Prepare / Review estimated tax payments
• Support income tax accounting and reporting under US GAAP for SEC reporting purposes
• Support international tax controversy teams on tax audits
• Work with other stakeholders, including Accounting and Transfer Pricing teams
• Assist with process improvement and automation implementation throughout the foreign tax reporting and compliance process, including use of Artificial Intelligence
• Support regular reporting of key metrics to Management
• Participate in special projects, as necessary
• Support international tax compliance and reporting aspects of mergers and acquisitions (due diligence and integration)

A day in the life
In this role you will support the EMEA International Tax Senior Manager group on the tax reporting (direct tax returns & tax accounting) as well as on international aspects of the Amazon's worldwide income tax provision. This position will also support the US and international tax controversy teams on audits and the international planning tax team on the compliance and reporting aspects of projects (including M&A and integration) and will work closely together with the EMEA Accounting teams.

About the team
The Amazon tax department is a fast-paced, team-focused, dynamic environment. The mission of the EMEA Foreign Reporting & Compliance team is to comply with foreign local tax reporting requirements and manage the worldwide, tax-related filings of Amazon’s foreign entities.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 6+ years of experience in chip design
- Hands-on experience in micro-architecture and RTL design (Verilog / System Verilog)
- Scripting expertise in C*, Perl, Python, or TCL
- BSc in Computer/Electrical Engineering
- Strong communication and collaboration skills

jobs.description:
AWS Utility Computing (UC) provides product innovations — from foundational services such as Amazon’s Simple Storage Service (S3) and Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2), to consistently released new product innovations that continue to set AWS’s services and features apart in the industry. As a member of the UC organization, you’ll support the development and management of Compute, Database, Storage, Internet of Things (IoT), Platform, and Productivity Apps services in AWS, including support for customers who require specialized security solutions for their cloud services.

Take part in the development of cutting-edge products within a disruptive system architecture. You will have the opportunity to work on the technologies that power the world’s largest cloud provider, in a dynamic, open, and fast-paced environment. AWS provides a highly reliable, scalable, low-cost infrastructure platform in the cloud, which powers hundreds of thousands of businesses in 190 countries around the world. Annapurna Labs, as part of Amazon Web Services (AWS), is looking for talented engineers to help us develop a semiconductor platform based on a revolutionary architecture.

Looking for exceptional senior engineers to join the top-tier team that is developing the next generation semiconductor platform, based on a revolutionary architecture. Engineers will participate in design activities, working on the next generation of our products.

You are invited to take part in developing, integrating and deploying cutting-edge technologies, starting with identification and definition of project requirements, architecture, feature development, and collaboration with the different groups.
Your design will be integrated into the nitro SoC, on millions of servers worldwide. This is an opportunity to have a large-scale impact.
As a VLSI engineer and a member of the Nitro project, you will have an impact over the device through its entire lifecycle, from the product definition stage to mass production. You will work in close collaboration with multiple groups, including Architecture, Software, Verification, Backend, and DFT.

Watch these videos to learn more about Annapurna Labs and the Nitro project:
AWS re:Invent 2025 - Keynote with Peter DeSantis and Dave Brown: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JeUpUK0nhC0
AWS re:Invent 2025 - Deep Dive into the AWS Nitro System https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cD1mNQ9YbeA

Key job responsibilities
*Full ownership of one or more IPs within the product:
-Micro-architecture
-RTL coding and debug
-Synthesis and timing closure
-Sign-off
* Supporting the Verification and Emulation teams: Test plan, Coverage review
* Ensuring that the chip meets quality and reliability standards
* Collaborating with cross-functional teams, including Product Definition, Verification, Software, and Physical design

About the team
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why flexible work hours and arrangements are part of our culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.
Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.
Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
AWS Global Sales drives adoption of the AWS cloud worldwide, enabling customers of all sizes to innovate and expand in the cloud. Our team empowers every customer to grow by providing tailored service, unmatched technology, and committed support. We dive deep to understand each customer's unique challenges, then craft innovative solutions that accelerate their success. This customer-first approach is how we built the world's most adopted cloud. Join us and help us grow.

Are you passionate about Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and GenerativeAI? Are you passionate about helping customers build solutions leveraging the state-of-the-art genAI/ML tools on Amazon Web Service (AWS)? Come join us!

At Amazon, we’ve been investing deeply in artificial intelligence for over 20 years, and many of the capabilities customers experience in our products are driven by machine learning. Amazon.com’s recommendations engine is driven by machine learning, as are the paths that optimize robotic picking routes in our fulfillment centers. Our supply chain, forecasting, and capacity planning are also informed by ML algorithms. Alexa is fueled by Natural Language Understanding and Automated Speech Recognition with deep learning; as is Prime Air. We have thousands of engineers at Amazon committed to machine learning and deep learning, and it’s a big part of our heritage.

Within AWS, we’re focused on bringing that knowledge and capability to customers through three layers of the AI stack: 1) Frameworks and Infrastructure with tools like Apache MxNet and TensorFlow, 2) Machine Learning Platforms such as Amazon SageMaker for data scientists, and, 3) API-driven Services like Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Lex, Amazon Kendra, Amazon Transcribe, Amazon Comprehend, and Amazon Rekognition to quickly add intelligence to applications with simple API calls.

AWS is looking for a genAI/ML Solutions Architect who will be the Subject Matter Expert for helping customers in the United States design solutions that leverage our GenAI services, including Amazon Bedrock, Amazon SageMaker, and Amazon Q. As part of the team, you will work closely with customers to enable large-scale use cases, design GenAI pipelines, and drive the adoption of AWS for the AI/ML platforms. You will interact with other SAs in the field, providing guidance on their customer engagements, and you will develop white papers, blogs, reference implementations, and presentations to enable customers to fully leverage genAI on AWS. Additionally, as the voice of the customer, you will work closely with the service teams, and submit product feature requests to drive the platform forward.

You must have deep technical experience working with technologies related to artificial intelligence, machine learning and/or deep learning. A strong mathematics and statistics background is preferred in addition to experience building machine learning models. You will be familiar with the ecosystem of software vendors in the genAI space, and will leverage this knowledge to help AWS customers in their selection process.

Travel up to 50% across the United States may be possible.

Key job responsibilities
Working with customers’ development and data science teams to deeply understand their business and technical needs. After understanding their needs, you will design solutions that make the best use of the AWS cloud platform and AWS AI/ML Services including SageMaker, Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Comprehend, Amazon Rekognition, Amazon Transcribe, Amazon Lex, Amazon Personalize, and the other AI/ML services.

Partner with SAs, Sales, Business Development and the AI/ML Service teams to accelerate customer adoption and revenue attainment in the AMERICAS for Amazon SageMaker and related services that support GenAI use cases.
Thought Leadership – Evangelize AWS ML services and share best practices through forums such as AWS blogs, whitepapers, reference architectures and public-speaking events such as AWS Summit, AWS re:Invent, etc.

Act as a technical liaison between customers and the AWS SageMaker or other service teams to provide customer driven product improvement feedback.
Develop and support an AWS internal community of GenAI related subject matter experts in the AMERICAS.

A day in the life
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the 8 description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

jobs.description:
Prime Video is disrupting the traditional television and movie industry with a massive library of high-quality media and reliable digital delivery. Our services are engineered for high availability, maximum reach, and cost savings we can pass on to customers. We enable the world to watch video instantly, from any device, whether on the road or from the comfort of a living room sofa.

Prime Video's Catalog & Data Platform (CDP) is the authoritative source of truth for every title available to hundreds of millions of customers across 249 territories — across Subscription and TransActional VOD, Channels, Linear, Live Sports and beyond. We drive the full content supply chain: from content and rights ingestion and processing, through product and offer distribution to every PV surface (Browse, Search, Fire TV, Web, Mobile, 3P Living Room devices, Google, Apple and other affiliates).
We are hiring an DE-II to help build the next generation of our Distribution platform. You will design and deliver systems that process billions of content requests and catalog queries daily, and ensure every customer in every territory sees the right content with the right offer at the right time. Your work directly impacts whether a customer in Mumbai can watch the latest season of a show, whether a partner in Tokyo can launch content across territories simultaneously, and whether Prime Video's storefront renders the correct experience for 100s of millions of active streamers worldwide.


The systems you will design and build are high performance distributed systems capable of enabling the growth of Prime Video global business. Innovation is key on this team and we are looking for technically savvy individuals with the ability to think big & deliver simple solutions to complex problems.

At Amazon, every employee has the opportunity to positively impact millions of customers, and you will be called on to identify and realize these opportunities. Our team works end-to-end through the Video platform, and our team members have direct ownership of the customer experience.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 10+ years of managing large complex facilities organizations experience
- 7+ years of senior management and leadership responsibilities experience

jobs.description:
Operations is at the heart of Amazon's business. We are known for our speed, accuracy, and exceptional service. Our buildings deliver tens of thousands of products to hundreds of countries worldwide, every day. The Reliability & Maintenance Engineering (RME) team are the business partners that work tirelessly behind the scenes to make it happen. We drive continuous improvement and maintain all the robotics and material handling equipment (MHE) to ensure our customers are met with the Amazon smile. Come join us on our journey!
About the Role:
As a Senior Maintenance Manager, you will transform workplace environments by leading site-based maintenance operations and developing the next generation of maintenance leaders. In this critical role, you'll oversee Maintenance Managers and their teams, develop strategic maintenance programs, and drive operational excellence across your site. You will blend strategic planning, financial management, and leadership development while navigating between managing preventative maintenance programs, coordinating with operations teams, and responding to emergencies that require quick, decisive action. You'll balance proactive planning with real-time problem resolution while fostering a culture of safety, innovation, and continuous improvement across your entire organization of 80+ team members.
What Do We Offer?
Amazon offers competitive compensation packages including comprehensive healthcare benefits starting on Day 1, matching 401(k) program, and up to 20 weeks of paid parental leave. But wait, there's more: We provide lightweight fire-resistant daily wear and composite toe safety shoes for your safety!


Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities include but are not limited to:
• You will provide leadership and mentorship to Maintenance Managers and Area Maintenance Managers, fostering professional growth, performance excellence, and developing bar-raising talent across your maintenance organization
• You will develop and maintain comprehensive preventative maintenance programs, manage site budgets and capital spending plans, and drive continuous improvement initiatives while maximizing equipment uptime and operational efficiency
• You will ensure rigorous adherence to safety protocols and procedures across all operational areas while partnering with site leadership to implement, audit, and enforce maintenance execution and safe work practices
• You will manage vendor relationships, coordinate external contractors, provide technical leadership on equipment end-of-life decisions using ROI analysis, and leverage Amazon's network size to maximize economies of scale
• You will travel domestically up to 15% of the time to support network-level initiatives and provide guidance across multiple sites
